name: Community Action Partnership, Huntsville/Madison & Limestone Counties, Inc
description_short: Non-profit community action agency providing LIHEAP utility assistance, housing support, and emergency services to low-income households in Huntsville/Madison & Limestone Counties, Alabama.
description_long:
Community Action Partnership Huntsville/Madison & Limestone Counties (CAPHMLC) is a federally-funded non-profit organization dedicated to helping low-income individuals and families achieve economic self-sufficiency. The agency operates under the National Community Action Partnership framework, which emphasizes changing lives, fostering hope, and improving communities through comprehensive social services.

CAPHMLC's primary offerings include the Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP), which provides utility deposit and bill payment assistance directly to utility companies and vendors, with eligibility-based assistance available up to four times annually. The organization also administers rental and mortgage assistance programs designed to prevent eviction and foreclosure for eligible households. Beyond housing and utilities, CAPHMLC provides emergency services on a case-by-case basis, including food vouchers, medical/dental care assistance, prescription medicine support, and transportation aid.

What distinguishes CAPHMLC is its comprehensive, holistic approach to poverty reduction. Beyond immediate financial assistance, the agency offers weatherization programs to reduce energy costs long-term, nutrition support, transportation services, and GED educational programs. This multi-faceted service model reflects their mission to mobilize federal, state, and local resources for advocacy and economic development rather than simply providing emergency relief.

The organization is legitimate and operates transparently, with documented board governance, staff accountability, and published agency reports. However, prospective clients should understand that all assistance is subject to eligibility requirements and available funding, which can fluctuate. Emergency assistance is determined case-by-case, meaning approval is not guaranteed. Service availability may also vary by location within the three-county service area.
pros: Provides LIHEAP utility assistance directly to vendors, preventing service interruptions for eligible low-income households
Offers rental and mortgage assistance specifically designed to prevent eviction and foreclosure
Includes emergency assistance programs covering food, medical care, dental care, and prescription medicine
Provides weatherization programs to reduce long-term energy costs beyond one-time bill assistance
Multi-service approach includes GED programs and transportation services for economic mobility
Assistance available up to four times per year for utilities based on eligibility
Non-profit structure with published reports and transparent governance
cons: All assistance requires eligibility verification and may be limited by available funding
Emergency assistance determined case-by-case with no guaranteed approval
Service limited to three-county region (Huntsville/Madison & Limestone Counties only)
Website lacks specific income thresholds or eligibility criteria, requiring phone contact for details
No indication of average processing times or wait periods for assistance approval
best_for: Low-income households struggling with utility bills or utility deposits in the Huntsville/Madison/Limestone Counties area
Families facing imminent eviction or foreclosure seeking rental or mortgage assistance
Individuals and families experiencing emergency situations requiring food, medical, or transportation assistance
Low-income residents seeking long-term energy cost reduction through weatherization programs
diagnosis: CAPHMLC is best for low-income households in their three-county service area facing immediate utility, housing, or emergency crises. The primary caveat is that all assistance depends on eligibility verification and current funding availability—approval is not automatic, and emergency services are evaluated case-by-case. Contact 256-851-9800 to confirm eligibility before applying.
services: LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program) utility deposit assistance
LIHEAP utility bill payment assistance (up to 4 times per year)
Emergency medical and dental care assistance
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Non-profit providing utility assistance, housing support, and emergency services to low-income households in Huntsville/Madison and Limestone Counties.
